word_list.word: a good Jack makes a good Jill
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɡˈʊd dʒˈæk mˌeɪks ɐ ɡˈʊd dʒˈɪl

senses.definition: used to imply that a person's positive qualities and behavior can have a positive influence on their partner

examples.x: After years of being together, they had both become better people, and they knew that it was because a good Jack makes a good Jill - their positive qualities had rubbed off on each other over time

senses.definition: used to imply that a husband's happiness is closely linked to the happiness of his wife, and taking care of her needs and wants is important for a fulfilling marriage

word_list.word: he that will thrive must first ask his wife
word_list.pronunciation: hiː ðæt wɪl θɹˈaɪv mˈʌst fˈɜːst ˈæsk hɪz wˈaɪf

senses.definition: used to imply that a husband and wife's partnership is essential for achieving success, and that the husband's willingness to listen to and consider his wife's opinion can lead to more favorable outcomes

examples.x: As a newly married couple, we have learned that he that will thrive must first ask his wife, and we always make sure to consult each other before making any important decisions.

word_list.word: marry in haste, repent at leisure
word_list.pronunciation: mˈæɹi ɪn hˈeɪst ɹɪpˈɛnt æt lˈiːʒɚ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that rushing into a marriage without careful consideration can lead to regret and long-term consequences

word_list.word: a young man married is a young man marred
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ jˈʌŋ mˈæn mˈæɹɪd ɪz ɐ jˈʌŋ mˈæn mˈɑːɹd

senses.definition: used to imply that marrying at a young age can be detrimental to a person's personal and professional development, as it can create additional responsibilities and limit opportunities

examples.x: The man had gotten married at a young age and struggled to balance his personal and professional responsibilities, recognizing that a young man married is a young man marred.

word_list.word: honest men marry quickly, wise men not at all
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɑːnɪst mˈɛn mˈæɹi kwˈɪkli wˈaɪz mˈɛn nˌɑːt æt ˈɔːl

senses.definition: used to suggest that straightforward and sincere men tend to get married early in life, while more prudent men may choose to remain unmarried

word_list.pronunciation: fˈɜːst θɹˈaɪv ænd ɔːɹ ðˈɛn wˈaɪv

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is wise to achieve financial stability and independence before committing to the responsibilities of marriage and family

word_list.word: better be an old man's darling than (to be|) a young man's slave
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚ biː ɐn ˈoʊld mˈænz dˈɑːɹlɪŋ ðɐn təbi ɔːɹ ɐ jˈʌŋ mˈænz slˈeɪv

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is better to be cherished and valued by an older person than to be under the control and mistreatment of a younger person

word_list.word: better wed over the mixen than over the moor
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚ wˈɛd ˌoʊvɚ ðə mˈɪksən ðɐn ˌoʊvɚ ðə mˈʊɹ

senses.definition: used to advise that it is wiser to marry someone who lives nearby, even if they are not perfect, than to seek a perfect partner far away in a remote place

word_list.word: a rouk-town's seldom a good housewife at home
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɹˈaʊktˈaʊnz sˈɛldəm ɐ ɡˈʊd hˈaʊswaɪf æt hˈoʊm

senses.definition: used to suggest that someone who spends a lot of time gossiping or meddling in other people's affairs is unlikely to be a good homemaker or caretaker of their own household

word_list.word: a good husband makes a good wife
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɡˈʊd hˈʌsbənd mˌeɪks ɐ ɡˈʊd wˈaɪf

senses.definition: used to imply that a woman's behavior and character are influenced by the way her husband treats her, emphasizing that both parties are important in creating a successful marriage

examples.x: Anna always believed that a good husband makes a good wife, and treated her own husband with love and respect, which in turn helped to create a strong and successful marriage.

word_list.word: never marry for money, but marry where money is
word_list.pronunciation: nˈɛvɚ mˈæɹi fɔːɹ mˈʌni bˌʌt mˈæɹi wˌɛɹ mˈʌni ɪz

senses.definition: used to suggest that one should not choose a life partner based solely on their wealth, but it is important to consider financial stability when choosing a partner
